evidences.content: # Stopgap measures to avoid government shutdown fail in Senate ## PBS NewsHour 5310000 subscribers 647 likes ### Description 62874 views Posted: 19 Sep 2025 House Republicans voted on their plan to avoid a government shutdown with a temporary, seven-week funding bill. But within hours, that was blocked by Democrats in the Senate. Their own plan for extending funding also fell short. So what does it mean? Here is the situation. Next week, congress is on recess. Congress has already left without recess. They can't come back Monday. Speaker Johnson and his team decided they will not be in session in the house the 29th or 30th. What this means is as you and I sit here, unless the senate passes the house bill passed today, there will be a government shutdown. And Democrats don't have that appetite. evidences.title: Background: U.S. appropriations process and continuing resolutions
evidences.content: Article One of the U.S. Constitution vests Congress with the authority to appropriate funds. Since 1977, the federal fiscal year runs from October 1 to September 30 and the budget process uses 12 individual spending bills that must be passed. Political polarization has affected this process, often forcing lawmakers to pass continuing resolutions to temporarily fund the government.
